title: B.Sc. (Hons with Research) Forensic Science with specialization in Forensic Physics and Ballistics
content: The B.Sc. (Hons. with Research) Forensic Science with specialization in Forensic Physics and Ballistics is a specialized program designed for students interested in the scientific principles behind physical evidence and firearm-related investigations. This program offers a balanced blend of theoretical knowledge and hands-on training in forensic physics, ballistics, impact analysis, and crime scene reconstruction. Students engage in practical learning through advanced laboratories equipped with modern instrumentation for firearms examination, trajectory analysis, tool mark identification, and material characterization. The curriculum encourages scientific inquiry and precision, with a strong research component that allows students to undertake independent projects on real-world forensic challenges. Graduates of this program are prepared for careers in forensic laboratories, police and defense services, ballistic units, investigative agencies, and private security and consulting firms. The degree also lays a strong foundation for further research and higher studies in applied physics, ballistics, and forensic science. With increasing reliance on scientific methods in criminal investigations and national security, this specialization provides students with the technical skills, analytical mindset, and research capabilities required to excel in the evolving field of forensic science. Students with a CUET / SAT (DI Code - 71057 ) score will be given preference in the merit list 1) B.Sc (Hons/Hons with Research) in Forensic Science Ist yr. with minimum 50% marks or 5.0 CGPA on a 10 point scale 2) XII with Science with minimum 50% marks. 01 Core Scientific Foundation: Equip students with a strong grounding in physics, material science, and ballistics, integrated with forensic principles for crime scene analysis and evidence interpretation. 02 Specialized Technical Skills: Develop expertise in firearm examination, trajectory analysis, toolmark identification, and advanced instrumentation (e.g., spectroscopy, microscopy, and 3D imaging). 03 Research and Innovation: Cultivate analytical and research skills through hands-on projects, thesis work, and exposure to emerging technologies in forensic physics and ballistics. 04 Legal and Ethical Competence : Train students to present scientific findings effectively in court, adhere to ethical standards, and understand the legal framework governing ballistic evidence. 05 Industry and Career Preparedness: Prepare graduates for careers in law enforcement, forensic labs, defense research, or further studies through internships, case studies, and collaboration with forensic experts. 01 02 03 04 05 06 07 08 01 02 03 01 02 03 Students with a CUET / SAT (DI Code - 71057 ) score will be given preference in the merit list 1) B.Sc (Hons/Hons with Research) in Forensic Science Ist yr. with minimum 50% marks or 5.0 CGPA on a 10 point scale 2) XII with Science with minimum 50% marks.
